meta.review_field: biology/bioinformatics/cell-biology (ER+ breast cancer, BRD4 PROTAC, ChIP-seq)
meta.review_note: Source deterministic audit had 0 findings / 0 dropped_flags (all 24 claims abstained; only c8 planner-routed at T8). Deterministic verdicts preserved verbatim (DESIGN §3.1) -- none overridden, none synthesized. Review adjudicated every abstain: advisory (genuine concerns code can't settle, with recompute done first where a number existed -- c14 61.0% reproduced from the Venn, c3 0.70-not->0.7, c6 CI lower bound=1.0), subjective T8 routing, or reviewed-clean (bare abstain dropped, advisory:claims-auditor note left so the drop is auditable). Integrity screen (T7) found no duplication / digit-anomaly / tortured-phrasing signal to route. No bare abstains remain.

routed_to_human.note: tier T8: subjective/integrity — surfaced, not scored (DESIGN §3.5)
routed_to_human.quote: we first screened a panel of compounds and identified ZBC260 as the most potent degrader (Figure 3A)

routed_to_human.note: T2/T4 borderline over-statement, surfaced by recompute against the extracted figure values. Prose says correlation coefficients 'all exceeding 0.7', but the values read off Figures 1F-J (ev3) include a 0.70 -- which is NOT strictly >0.7 (min(0.71,0.70,0.72,0.75,0.77,0.83,0.77,0.73,0.77)=0.70). 'all >=0.7' holds; 'all exceeding 0.7' is off by the boundary case. Minor and figure-derived (ev3 confidence 0.6), so not a deterministic flag. Confirms-it: the exact correlation matrix / the 0.70 cell's true value at full precision.
routed_to_human.quote:
These co-localization events were supported by
strong correlation coefficients, all exceeding 0.7 (Figures 1F–J)

routed_to_human.note:
T2/T4 borderline-significance over-read. 'Elevated BRD4 expression also predicted poor prognosis in patients undergoing endocrine therapy' is supported by HR=1.4 with logrank p=0.047 -- but the reported 95% CI is (1, 1.95): the LOWER BOUND sits exactly on the null (HR=1). A CI whose lower limit is 1.0 paired with p just under 0.05 is the weakest tier of 'significant', and a directional prognosis claim leans on it as if firm. Not an arithmetic error (p<0.05 and CI are mutually consistent at the boundary), so no deterministic flag. Confirms-it: the un-rounded CI lower bound and event counts per arm; if the true lower bound is <=1.00 the association is not significant.
(Contrast c4 HR=1.61 CI (1.28-2.03) p=4.8e-05 and c5 HR=1.35 CI (1.04-1.75) p=0.023, both with CIs clearly excluding 1 -- those are clean.)
routed_to_human.quote:
elevated BRD4 expression also predicted poor
prognosis in patients undergoing endocrine therapy (Figure 2C)

routed_to_human.note: T3 quantification gap. 'Dose- and time-dependent' (implying monotonic decrease over 0-50 nM and 0-48 h) is asserted from Western blots at four dose points (0,5,25,50 nM) and four time points (0,12,24,48 h) with n=3 (ev10), but no densitometry, normalization to a loading control, or trend statistic is reported in the claim's evidence -- the monotonicity is read from band intensity by eye. Standard for a PROTAC degradation panel and very likely correct, but 'dependent' is a quantitative/causal word resting on a qualitative readout. Confirms-it: loading-control-normalized densitometry per lane with a monotonic-trend test (or hook-effect check at the top dose).
routed_to_human.quote:
ZBC260 also
led to a marked reduction in BRD4 protein levels in a dose- and
time-dependent manner (Figures 3C, D)

routed_to_human.note: Field-plausibility / evidence-strength note. 'BRD4 is significantly upregulated in the fulvestrant-resistant cell line' rests on a single re-analyzed public dataset (GSE118713) shown as a volcano plot (ev7), i.e. one resistant MCF7 line vs parental. Biologically coherent with the paper's BRD4-resistance thesis, but a one-dataset, one-cell-line differential-expression result is the kind of claim usually corroborated by protein-level data or a second resistance model before being stated as a general fact. Confirms-it: adjusted p / log2FC for BRD4 in GSE118713 and an orthogonal confirmation (qPCR/WB in the lab's own resistant line).
routed_to_human.quote:
BRD4 expression is
significantly upregulated in the fulvestrant-resistant cell line
(Figure 2D, Supplementary Table 1)

routed_to_human.note: T4 causal-chain over-reach. The claim packs three directional effects -- ZBC260 lowers BRD4 occupancy on the GREB1 promoter, ZBC260+fulvestrant lowers it further, and estrogen RESCUES it -- onto one ChIP-qPCR bar graph (Fig 8B, n=3, p<0.05; ev21). The 'rescue' step in particular is a mechanistic causal statement; pairwise significance across four conditions does not by itself establish the ordered chain or the rescue direction without the relevant contrasts. Plausible given the ER/estrogen axis, but the wording outruns a single-locus n=3 assay. Confirms-it: per-contrast statistics (vehicle vs ZBC260; ZBC260 vs +Ful; ZBC260 vs +E2) with the specific comparison and correction stated.
routed_to_human.quote:
ZBC260
induce BRD4 less chromatin binding events on the promoter
regions of GREB1, and ZBC260 combined with fulvestrant more
impair the events, but estrogen rescue the events (Figure 8B)

routed_to_human.note: T3 'significant' without a reported test. All five factors' promoter binding densities are said to be 'significantly elevated' in MCF7 vs MCF10A (Figs 1A-E, ev1, TSS +/- 5 kb metagene profiles), but no test, n, or p accompanies the comparison in the claim's evidence -- 'significantly' reads as visual rather than statistical. Comparing aggregate ChIP-seq density between two cell lines is also sensitive to read-depth / spike-in normalization, which the claim does not address. Likely directionally right. Confirms-it: the normalization scheme and a quantitative test (e.g. per-gene density distributions with an effect size / p) rather than landscape overlays.
routed_to_human.quote:
the chromatin
binding densities of all five factors were significantly elevated in
promoter regions of MCF7 cells compared to MCF10A cells

routed_to_human.note: Whether singling out GREB1 -- as THE core BRD4/ER-shared, super-enhancer-driven effector whose combined ZBC260+fulvestrant suppression matters therapeutically -- is an important and novel contribution (vs GREB1 already being a well-known estrogen-induced ER target) is an irreducibly subjective significance/novelty judgement LITMUS does not score (DESIGN §3.5, §5 T8). The underlying suppression is empirically checkable (see the claims-auditor note on c19); only its IMPORTANCE is routed. For an editor / domain reviewer.
routed_to_human.quote: combined ZBC260 and fulvestrant treatment potently inhibited the expression of GREB1 (Figures 6F, G)

routed_to_human.note: Reviewed, no over-reach found. The '>=78%' summary is exactly consistent with the four stated overlaps (min(93.93,88.42,83.18,78.00)=78.00), so the bound is faithful, not inflated. The percentages themselves are figure-derived (Figs 1F-J) and not independently recomputable from the graph here. Reviewed, no over-reach found. Internal count/percentage claim, correctly scoped to its figure/table; no claim-vs-evidence over-statement. No numeric verifier covers it yet (synthesis candidate, DESIGN §8, WS-F); numeric recompute deferred to a synthesized T0/T2 verifier.
routed_to_human.quote:
a substantial majority (≥78%) of
BRD4 binding sites coincided with histone acetylation sites, specifically H3K27ac (93.93%), H3K4ac (88.42%), H3K9ac (83.18%), and
H3K23ac (78.00%)

routed_to_human.note: Reviewed and RECOMPUTED clean. The '61.0%' is reproducible from the Fig 6A Venn counts in ev14: overlap/(overlap+ER_only) = 5951/(5951+3812) = 60.95% ~ 61.0% (and the Venn's own 43.2/34.6/22.2 percentages reproduce exactly from 7416/5951/3812). Prose, figure label, and arithmetic agree -- no over-reach. A T0/T2 overlap-percentage verifier would confirm this deterministically; none is wired up yet (DESIGN §8).
routed_to_human.quote: 61.0% of ER promoter-binding genes overlapped with BRD4

routed_to_human.note: Reviewed, no over-reach found. Approximate wording ('over 60%') is appropriately hedged relative to the exact figure. Reviewed, no over-reach found. Internal count/percentage claim, correctly scoped to its figure/table; no claim-vs-evidence over-statement. No numeric verifier covers it yet (synthesis candidate, DESIGN §8, WS-F); numeric recompute deferred to a synthesized T0/T2 verifier.
routed_to_human.quote:

promoter-binding events and 50% of its transcriptome overlap with
that of BRD4

routed_to_human.note: Reviewed and RECOMPUTED clean. 'High BRD4 expression associated with reduced survival (HR=1.61)' is internally coherent: the reported 95% CI (1.28-2.03) excludes 1 and logrank p=4.8e-05 << 0.05 agree on a significant adverse association (ev4, KM Fig 2A). Direction and strength of the prose match the statistic -- no over-reach. (Contrast the borderline c6.) A T2/T1 HR<->CI<->p coherence verifier would confirm deterministically; none is wired up yet (DESIGN §8).
routed_to_human.quote:
a high expression level of BRD4 was significantly
associated with reduced survival probability in breast cancer
patients

routed_to_human.note: Reviewed and RECOMPUTED clean. 'High BRD4 predicts poor prognosis in luminal subtype (HR=1.35)' is coherent: CI (1.04-1.75) excludes 1 and logrank p=0.023<0.05 agree (ev5, KM Fig 2B). Modest but genuinely significant; the claim is scoped to the luminal subtype and not over-extended. HR<->CI<->p coherence verifier deferred (DESIGN §8).
routed_to_human.quote:
including those with the luminal subtype (Figures 2A,
B)

routed_to_human.note: Reviewed, no over-reach found. The MG132 rescue of ZBC260-induced degradation is the standard proteasome-dependence control for a PROTAC; the claim states exactly what the blot shows (Fig 3B) and does not over-extend. Reviewed, no over-reach found. Qualitative within-paper effect, correctly described and not over-generalized beyond the figure it rests on. No deterministic verifier covers it yet (synthesis candidate, DESIGN §8); review clears it of claim-vs-evidence over-statement.
routed_to_human.quote:
pre-treatment with the proteasome inhibitor
MG132 abrogated ZBC260-induced BRD4 degradation
(Figure 3B)

routed_to_human.note: Reviewed, no over-reach found. 'ZBC260 enhanced fulvestrant's effect on migration / proliferation / apoptosis' is supported by the labelled p<0.05 contrasts in Fig 4B-E (ev11: # marks Ful+ZBC260 vs Ful), i.e. the combination-vs-single comparison the claim actually makes. Not over-stated. Reviewed, no over-reach found. Qualitative within-paper effect, correctly described and not over-generalized beyond the figure it rests on. No deterministic verifier covers it yet (synthesis candidate, DESIGN §8); review clears it of claim-vs-evidence over-statement.
routed_to_human.quote:
ZBC260 enhanced the inhibitory effect of fulvestrant to
more profoundly inhibit cell migration and proliferation and to
induce apoptosis (Figures 4B–E)

routed_to_human.note: Reviewed, no over-reach found (empirics only; IMPORTANCE routed separately as subjective:significance). 'Combined ZBC260+fulvestrant potently inhibited GREB1' is supported by the immunofluorescence panel (Fig 6F-G, ev19) and stated as a within-paper observation, not a clinical claim. Reviewed, no over-reach found. Qualitative within-paper effect, correctly described and not over-generalized beyond the figure it rests on. No deterministic verifier covers it yet (synthesis candidate, DESIGN §8); review clears it of claim-vs-evidence over-statement.
routed_to_human.quote: combined ZBC260 and fulvestrant treatment potently inhibited the expression of GREB1 (Figures 6F, G)

routed_to_human.note: Reviewed, no over-reach found. 'GREB1 knockdown enhanced ZBC260's effect on migration / proliferation / apoptosis' restates the Fig 7A-D comparisons (ev20, si-GREB1+ZBC260 vs ZBC260); a clean epistatic/additivity statement, not over-generalized. Reviewed, no over-reach found. Qualitative within-paper effect, correctly described and not over-generalized beyond the figure it rests on. No deterministic verifier covers it yet (synthesis candidate, DESIGN §8); review clears it of claim-vs-evidence over-statement.
routed_to_human.quote:
GREB1 knock down also enhanced the
inhibitory effect of ZBC260 to more profoundly inhibit cell migration and proliferation and to induce apoptosis (Figures 7A–D)

routed_to_human.note: Reviewed, field-plausible, no over-reach. Background claim that ER+ disease is ~75% of breast cancers and acquired resistance drives relapse in up to 30% (Discussion, cited ref 7; ev22). Both figures are consistent with established clinical epidemiology and are presented as cited background, not as this study's finding. A T5/T1 literature-citation verifier (does ref 7 support 75% / 30%?) is the right check; not built yet (DESIGN §5 T5).
routed_to_human.quote:
which accounts for about 75% of all
cases, acquired resistance drives relapse in up to 30% of patients (7)
